meta.note: March 19 run — Guardian mandatory AI training + in-house tools (all staff, March 4 — not previously captured); iconik Web 26.03 confirmed shipped; Digiday Publishing Summit next week (Guardian presenting: zero-click, AI licensing, RAG readiness); LucidLink+Frame.io GA window closes March 31; NAB BEIT full schedule confirmed (30 days)

decisionPanels.title: 🧠 Guardian Mandatory AI Training + In-House Tools — MMR AI Governance Alignment (URGENT)
decisionPanels.summary: The Guardian published an updated AI policy on March 4 (journalism.co.uk coverage confirmed). Key facts: (1) ALL staff now required to complete mandatory AI training — designed to help everyone understand how AI works and use it responsibly, and will evolve as the technology develops. (2) Guardian is building IN-HOUSE AI tools aligned with editorial standards — specifically: image description writing, archive search, document analysis, and audio transcription — all with built-in guardrails to protect authenticity and values. (3) Transparency requirement: any significant GenAI use in journalism must be clearly labelled. (4) Editorial code and AI principles revised. This is directly relevant to MMR: the in-house tools being built (transcription, archive search) are EXACTLY what iconik already delivers. MMR's AI governance documentation needs to align with this institutional policy NOW, before go-live.
actions: READ the Guardian's updated AI principles (theguardian.com/help/insideguardian/2026/mar/04/how-the-guardian-is-using-genai) and align MMR's AI governance section with the language. Use it as the source of truth for how to frame iconik's Analytical AI (safe: metadata, transcription, search) vs any future Generative AI use.

LEVERAGE the mandatory training: all staff going through AI training in 2026 creates a window for MMR onboarding to reference the institutional framing. Producers learning 'how AI works' through Guardian training will be more receptive to iconik AI features if MMR docs use the same language.

NOTE the in-house Guardian archive search + transcription tools being built — these could interface with iconik via API. Worth surfacing to the Guardian product/engineering team whether MMR's iconik setup should be an API endpoint for these tools.
ADD transparency labelling requirement to MMR documentation: any AI-generated metadata in iconik (auto-tags, AI transcripts) should be clearly distinguished from human-validated metadata.

decisionPanels.title: 📦 iconik Web 26.03 Shipped + LucidLink+Frame.io GA Window: 12 Days
decisionPanels.summary: Two time-critical vendor signals: (1) iconik Web 26.03 has shipped — confirmed in the Backlight help centre navigation breadcrumbs (sitting above 26.02 in Q1 2026 release sequence). Content was behind auth (403) so specific features are unconfirmed, but a new quarterly release is live. Also confirmed: Iconik iOS 1.3.1 is available. Guardian should verify what 26.03 contains and whether it's live on their instance. (2) LucidLink + Frame.io integration GA is targeted for Q1 2026 — Q1 ends March 31, which is 12 days from tonight. The integration turns Frame.io Camera to Cloud into 'Camera to Timeline' — field footage appears instantly in LucidLink Filespace and directly in Premiere/After Effects without downloading or relinking. Included with Business and Enterprise plans. If confirmed GA before March 31, this is an immediate workflow upgrade for Guardian field production teams.
actions: CHECK: Log into Guardian's iconik instance — is 26.03 showing? Review release notes in-app. Confirm what new features have shipped (the navigation confirms a new release exists).

CONTACT LucidLink account team: confirm Frame.io integration GA status. Q1 closes March 31 — if it's live now or this week, Guardian field teams can use it immediately for Camera to Timeline workflows.
ALSO CHECK: Iconik iOS 1.3.1 — what's new? Mobile app updates often include producer-facing features (share, review, upload). Check before updating producer training materials.
VERIFY iconik 26.02 multi-channel audio is live on Guardian instance (action carried from March 18) — combine this check with the 26.03 verification into a single login session.

decisionPanels.title: 📊 Digiday Publishing Summit (March 23-25): Guardian Presenting — RAG Readiness Emerging
decisionPanels.summary: Next week (March 23-25, Vail, Colorado), Guardian executives join Bloomberg, Washington Post, Business Insider, The Atlantic, and others at the Digiday Publishing Summit. Key session topics: zero-click audience strategy, AI content licensing and RAG readiness, creator network development for younger audience engagement. The term 'RAG readiness' (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) is new — publishers are now structuring their archives specifically to be retrieved and cited by AI assistants and chatbots, not just indexed by search engines. This is the practical follow-on to 'AEO' (Answer Engine Optimisation) and 'liquid content.' It means that how Guardian content is structured, described, and versioned in iconik directly determines whether it's AI-retrievable. MMR's metadata schema is therefore also Guardian's AI distribution layer.
actions: TRACK the Digiday summit coverage next week (March 24-26): what is Guardian presenting on zero-click strategy? This is the most direct signal available about Guardian's off-platform content posture in 2026.

ADD 'RAG readiness' to the framing of MMR's iconik metadata schema: structured metadata (topics, entities, people, events, locations, format, language, date) makes content AI-retrievable. This is now a business requirement, not a nice-to-have.

NOTE: AI licensing is a live Guardian strategic discussion (SPUR coalition + summit presentation). MMR documentation should include fields for content licensing status and AI usage permissions — these are becoming standard MAM requirements.
WATCH for any post-summit reporting on what Guardian's leadership said about zero-click and platform strategy.

decisionPanels.title: 🏭 NAB 2026 BEIT: Full Schedule Confirmed — 30 Days (April 18-21)
decisionPanels.summary: NAB's Broadcast Engineering and IT Conference full schedule is now confirmed. April 18-21 at the Las Vegas Convention Center. Programme: cloud-native architectures, IP-based infrastructures, AI-driven workflows, NextGen TV, and 'how audiences engage with broadcast and digital media.' Poppy Crum keynote April 18 ('From Data to Mind: How Technology is Reshaping Storytellers and Media Consumers') anchors the neuroscience-meets-media theme. SMPTE VIBE session on AI-Driven Live Production confirmed for April 20 (1:30-2:30pm). Vendor lineup: Backlight (N2829), Harmonic Spectrum X Plus (W2831), AWS Elemental Inference (W1701), Avid AI, EditShare FLOW AI (N1251), Tedial AI MAM. The BEIT session on 'NextGen TV' is new intel — this covers ATSC 3.0 and broadcast-OTT convergence, relevant if Guardian ever extends to broadcast distribution. NAB is now 30 days away.
actions: DECISION: NAB 2026 attendance. 30 days. BEIT schedule confirms April 18-21 includes all priority sessions. A two-day window (April 20-21) covers the AI-Driven Live Production session + Backlight + Harmonic + AWS Elemental vendor meetings.

PREP vendor question list for NAB: (1) iconik 26.03 features + roadmap; (2) LucidLink Frame.io GA status + Connect/S3 pricing; (3) AWS Elemental Inference pricing for news publishers; (4) Harmonic Spectrum X Plus live news ingest capabilities; (5) Tedial AI MAM — how does it differ from iconik?
REGISTER if attending — standard registration open. Book flights + hotel now for April 18-22.

Wildmoka supports publishing to social drafts (Facebook/Instagram drafts, YouTube unlisted) — batch social scheduling workflow available now for Guardian social teams without live-publish risk

Guardian Studios: Leah Green (ex-Channel 4) as head — personality-led journalism, video-first mandate; Project Launcher must support journalist-as-creator social publishing workflows
Guardian US 30M audience (+21% YoY, Nov 2025 Comscore): highest US traffic of any UK newspaper brand — US audience is the growth engine; YouTube is the distribution layer
Chartbeat 2026 publishing strategy (March 17): AI-assisted headlines deliver 32% CTR lift vs 6% for non-AI; social video + multiple page visits = engagement engine; loyal readers drive recirculation
Meta Creator Fast Track LAUNCHED (March 18, 2026): $1,000/month for 100K+ followers, $3,000/month for 1M+ followers — poaching TikTok/YouTube creators to Facebook Reels; 3 months guaranteed pay

Social search as new discoverability layer (March 2026): TikTok for recommendations, YouTube for tutorials, Reddit for opinions — AEO + social search visibility now more important than Google SEO alone
INMA March 2026: YouTube CEO 'Creators are the new studios' — publishers competing against self-funded creators; YouTube UK's #2 streaming service (behind BBC, ahead of ITV)
DCN March 12: publishers now actively hiring YouTube-specialist video producers — YouTube climbing priority lists in 2026 as AI cuts search referral traffic
Reuters Institute 2026: YouTube main publisher focus (+74 net score); TikTok +56, Instagram +41; publishers deprioritising Facebook (-23) and X (-52); search traffic -40% over 3 years

Counter-trend signal (Metricool 2026): long-form video + authentic storytelling returning — audiences seeking 'more human rhythm' after short-form AI content saturation; Guardian's editorial depth is a differentiator
YouTube March 13: live stream → instant vertical layout in live control room — zero-cost Shorts from any live feed, zero engineering required

AWS Elemental Inference (GA): AI during encoding (not after) — 9:16 vertical at 6-10s latency from live feeds; Fox Sports cut turnaround from 45-60 min to <15 min; demoing at NAB W1701
Tedial AI MAM at NAB 2026: 'intelligent media lifecycle' platform — competitor to iconik worth evaluating at NAB to benchmark Guardian's stack

Reuters Institute 2026: 'liquid content' + AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) + search -40% — MMR iconik metadata schema must support format-agnostic liquid content packaging from creation; RAG readiness = structured metadata at ingest
EditShare FLOW AI + MediaSilo AI at NAB 2026 (N1251): Analytical AI for auto-metadata (speech, faces, scenes, text) — new MediaSilo bundles with unlimited AI and forensic watermarking
